rag.description: The SwitchParentFrame action switches WebDriver context one level up from the current child frame to its direct parent frame by calling `WebDriver.SwitchTo().ParentFrame()` unconditionally. There are no parameters, no properties, and no branching — the implementation is a single unconditional call followed by returning a standard PluginResponseModel with no extra output data. If the driver is already at default content (not inside any frame), behavior is driver-dependent and typically raises a W3C-level error.

qa.question: What does SwitchParentFrame do?
qa.answer: It calls `WebDriver.SwitchTo().ParentFrame()`, moving the WebDriver context exactly one level up from the currently active child frame to its immediate parent frame.

qa.question: Does SwitchParentFrame accept any parameters or properties?
qa.answer: No. The plugin has no parameters and no properties. It always performs a single unconditional call to `WebDriver.SwitchTo().ParentFrame()`.

qa.question: Does SwitchParentFrame produce any output parameters?
qa.answer: No. The plugin returns `this.NewPluginResponse()` with no additional payload. There are no output parameters or session-data entries added to the response.

qa.question: What happens if SwitchParentFrame is called when the driver is already at default content (not inside any frame)?
qa.answer: Behavior is driver-dependent. Most WebDriver implementations throw a W3C-level error because there is no parent frame above default content. The plugin does not guard against this condition.

qa.question: Does SwitchParentFrame move to default content or only one level up?
qa.answer: It moves exactly one level up in the frame hierarchy — to the immediate parent frame. If nested more than one level deep, multiple sequential calls are required to reach default content.

qa.question: What alias is available for SwitchParentFrame?
qa.answer: SwitchParentFrame is also accepted as SwitchToParentFrame — both resolve to identical behavior.

qa.question: What is the manifest version for SwitchParentFrame?
What W3C WebDriver endpoint does SwitchParentFrame use?
qa.answer: SwitchParentFrame uses the W3C WebDriver POST /session/{session id}/frame/parent endpoint.

qa.question: Does SwitchParentFrame accept any Argument, Locator, or OnElement?
qa.answer: No. SwitchParentFrame has no parameters and no properties. It always performs a single unconditional call to WebDriver.SwitchTo().ParentFrame() with no configuration.

description: ### Purpose
SwitchParentFrame switches WebDriver context one level up from the current child frame to its direct parent frame by calling `WebDriver.SwitchTo().ParentFrame()` unconditionally.
It requires no parameters or configuration and always performs a single deterministic switch.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature          | Description                                                                                                             |
| Switch to Parent | Calls `WebDriver.SwitchTo().ParentFrame()` unconditionally, moving context exactly one level up in the frame hierarchy. |
### Usages in RPA
| Usage            | Description                                                                                   |
| Frame Navigation | Switches context back to the parent frame after completing work inside a child frame.         |
| Data Extraction  | Returns the driver to the parent frame so content outside the child frame can be accessed.    |
| Form Interaction | Allows bots to interact with parent-frame elements after completing actions in a child frame. |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Usage                | Description                                                                                                   |
| UI Testing           | Enables tests to navigate back to the parent frame for assertions or actions outside the current child frame. |
| Frame Verification   | Confirms that frame switching correctly restores context to the parent frame during test execution.           |
| Multi-Frame Handling | Supports step-by-step traversal of nested frame hierarchies by repeatedly switching up one level at a time.   |

edge_cases: Driver is at default content — calling `SwitchTo().ParentFrame()` when not inside any frame is driver-dependent and typically throws a W3C error; the plugin does not guard against this condition
Nested frames deeper than one level — the plugin moves exactly one level up; reaching default content from a deeply nested frame requires multiple sequential invocations of SwitchParentFrame
Parent frame content has changed since entry — the driver context is valid after the switch, but the parent DOM state may differ from what it was when the child frame was entered
Frame hierarchy was restructured by JavaScript — if the parent frame's structure changed while inside the child frame, element references held before the switch may be stale or invalid

Calling after SwitchTo().DefaultContent() — that call resets context to top-level document, not a frame; calling SwitchParentFrame afterward is equivalent to calling it from default content and will likely throw
annotations.expected_result: WebDriver context moves one level up from the active child frame to its direct parent frame.
annotations.notes: No Argument, Locator, or OnElement values are needed. The switch is unconditional and applies to whatever frame context the driver is currently in.

description: ### Switch from a child frame back to its parent frame
Switch WebDriver context one level up from the currently active child frame to its direct parent frame.
No configuration is required — the plugin calls `WebDriver.SwitchTo().ParentFrame()` unconditionally.

edge_cases: Three levels deep — three sequential calls to SwitchParentFrame are required to return to default content
Intermediate frame has been removed by JavaScript — switching to its parent may raise a StaleElementReferenceException or similar error
Parent frame content changed during child frame work — element references obtained before entering the child frame may be stale after returning
annotations.expected_result: WebDriver context moves up two levels from a doubly nested child frame to the top-level parent frame by invoking SwitchParentFrame twice in sequence.
annotations.notes: Each SwitchParentFrame call moves exactly one level up. For a frame nested two levels deep, two sequential invocations are required to return to default content.

description: ### Traverse up multiple frame levels
Invoke SwitchParentFrame twice in sequence to move from a doubly nested child frame up to the top-level document context.
Each invocation moves exactly one level up, so the number of calls required equals the nesting depth.

summary: SwitchParentFrame switches WebDriver context one level up from the current child frame to its direct parent frame by calling `WebDriver.SwitchTo().ParentFrame()` unconditionally.
It requires no parameters or properties and performs a single deterministic switch with no branching.
In RPA it enables efficient frame traversal after working in child frames; in automation testing it restores context to the parent frame for assertions or further interactions.
